scope: PyTorch MFU speedups for next ALM 14B training round (r6 or later)

checklist.evidence: training/train_alm_14b.py:168-180 — from_pretrained() has no attn_implementation kwarg. No grep matches for flash_attn / sdpa_kernel / attn_implementation / scaled_dot_product_attention anywhere in .py files.
checklist.current_behavior: HF defaults to 'eager' attention on Qwen2 for seq<=4096 when flash-attn package is not explicitly requested. Attention is O(n^2) materialized, not fused.
checklist.recommendation: attn_implementation='flash_attention_2' in AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained (both distributed and non-distributed branches). flash-attn>=2.6 wheels ship on RunPod pytorch:2.4.0; FA3 (H100 Hopper) only lands in flash-attn 2.7+ and is still opt-in (FLASH_ATTENTION_FORCE_FA3=1 / use_fa3=True) — not stable for Qwen2 yet, so FA2 is the safe default.
checklist.uplift_notes: seq=1024 at bf16: FA2 fuses softmax+matmul, saves HBM on attention scores; typical MFU gain 25-40% for 14B-class models on H100 vs eager. Conservative 30%.

checklist.evidence: No grep matches for torch.compile in any .py file under training/.
checklist.current_behavior: Model runs in eager mode; every step re-launches ~1500 CUDA kernels.
checklist.recommendation: torch.compile(model, mode='max-autotune', dynamic=False, fullgraph=False) right before the FSDP wrap. Must be applied AFTER get_peft_model and BEFORE FSDP() when both are used (Dynamo tracing through FSDP shards is brittle as of torch 2.4). With seq=1024 fixed and batch=1 fixed, static shapes are guaranteed → dynamic=False is safe.
checklist.uplift_notes: Conservative: LoRA+FSDP+bf16 stack gets 10-18% from max-autotune on H100. PEFT adapter graph breaks can cap gains; fallback to mode='reduce-overhead' if compile hangs >3min.

checklist.evidence: training/train_alm_14b.py:258-264 — torch.optim.AdamW(..., foreach=False) explicitly. No fused=True, no apex FusedAdam import.
checklist.current_behavior: per-param Python loop in optimizer.step(); on H100 with only LoRA params (~50M) this is 5-8ms/step of pure Python overhead.
checklist.recommendation: fused=True on CUDA. Keep foreach as fallback (False is actively worse than foreach=True, but fused supersedes both). Requires torch>=2.0 which is satisfied.
checklist.uplift_notes: Small absolute gain because LoRA params are tiny, but free and risk-free.

checklist.evidence: train_alm_14b.py:170,177,223-225,306,354 — bf16 throughout (model weights, autocast fwd, autocast eval, FSDP MixedPrecision param/reduce/buffer).
checklist.current_behavior: bf16 native — correct choice for 14B on H100. fp8 via transformer_engine would net another ~30% but is out of scope (requires H100 Transformer Engine install, Qwen2 patching, and is unstable with LoRA).
checklist.recommendation: KEEP bf16. Do NOT add fp8 for r5/r6 — that is a separate experimental track.

checklist.current_behavior: Activation checkpointing is ON. This is a ~25-30% throughput penalty in exchange for memory headroom.
checklist.recommendation: For 14B LoRA on 80GB H100 with batch=1 seq=1024: should be OFF. r5 memory budget comment says '~58 GB resident / 80 GB' which already has 22 GB headroom. With activation checkpoint OFF, peak adds ~8-12 GB (seq=1024 is small) → still fits. For 32B, keep ON.
checklist.uplift_notes: Biggest single gain. Must be validated with nvidia-smi at step 50 before running full 20k. If peak memory > 72 GB, revert to ON.

checklist.evidence: train_alm_14b.py:202-235 — FSDP FULL_SHARD with MixedPrecision(bf16), use_orig_params=True. Triggered only when --fsdp AND torchrun --nproc_per_node>1.
checklist.current_behavior: r5 single-H100 runs do NOT hit this path (uses device_map='auto' instead). FSDP is wired correctly for multi-GPU.
checklist.recommendation: For single-H100 r5/r6: FSDP irrelevant, skip. For future 2xH100 14B: FULL_SHARD is already correct. For 32B r1 (launch_alm_32b_r1.sh): likely needs SHARD_GRAD_OP + CPU offload — out of scope.
checklist.patch: N/A — already ON (single-GPU path bypasses it correctly)

checklist.evidence: train_alm_14b.py:252,255 — DataLoader(..., num_workers=0). No pin_memory, no persistent_workers, no prefetch_factor.
checklist.current_behavior: Main process does tokenization + batching + host-to-device copy inline. With 14B fwd/bwd taking 3+ sec/step, data loading is NOT the bottleneck — but pin_memory still reclaims ~0.5-1% via async H2D copy.
checklist.recommendation: num_workers=2, pin_memory=True, persistent_workers=True, prefetch_factor=2. Tokens are pre-tokenized in memory so num_workers=2 is ample.
checklist.uplift_notes: Tiny gain on this workload (compute-bound), but free. May matter more if seq is bumped to 4096.

checklist.evidence: launch_alm_14b_r5.sh / launch_alm_14b_r5_kbase.sh — no NCCL_* exports. Only launch_alm_32b_r1.sh sets NCCL_DEBUG and TORCH_NCCL_ASYNC_ERROR_HANDLING.
checklist.current_behavior: Single-H100 r5 does NO NCCL collectives (no torchrun), so NCCL env is moot for r5 specifically. For multi-GPU r6 it defaults to NCCL auto-tune which can leave P2P/IB perf on the table.
checklist.recommendation: Add NCCL_P2P_DISABLE=0, NCCL_IB_GID_INDEX=3, NCCL_NET_GDR_LEVEL=4, NCCL_ASYNC_ERROR_HANDLING=1, TORCH_NCCL_BLOCKING_WAIT=1 to the launcher — no-op on single GPU, correct on multi-GPU.
checklist.uplift_notes: Mostly insurance for multi-GPU r6. 0% impact on current single-H100 r5.

checklist.evidence: No torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32 or torch.backends.cudnn.benchmark = True in any training .py file. (bonus item beyond original checklist)
checklist.current_behavior: matmul defaults are fine for bf16 path (tf32 gates fp32 reductions), but cudnn.benchmark=True helps select optimal conv kernels — low-value on transformer but free.
checklist.recommendation: torch.set_float32_matmul_precision('high') + torch.backends.cudnn.benchmark = True at top of train().
checklist.uplift_notes: Bonus. Adds to flash_attention + compile pipeline, no conflict.

cumulative_uplift_caveats: Uplifts are NOT perfectly additive — flash_attention + disable_act_ckpt share the memory budget. Real cumulative MFU gain is typically 45-60% for this combination on H100.
Conservative combined estimate: +50% (e.g. 18.5 step/min → 28 step/min for r5 config).
MUST validate with nvidia-smi memory check at step 50 after disable_act_ckpt is applied.
application_order: 4. flash_attention (validate gen quality after — Qwen2 FA2 is well-tested but peft adapter dtype mismatches possible)
5. disable_act_ckpt (validate peak memory < 72 GB at step 50)
6. torch_compile (apply LAST — longest validation loop due to compile warmup time)
apply_to_next_run: r6 or r5_kbase (base-swap) whichever launches fresh first

sign_off.rule_never_resume_with_param_change: honored — all patches land on a NEW run from step 0
sign_off.rule_one_shot_best: honored — patches are prepared BEFORE next launch
sign_off.rule_no_quantization: honored — no bitsandbytes, no gptq, no awq
sign_off.rule_l0_untouched: honored — no files in anima/core/ modified
